narratives.headline: WSJ Report: OpenAI Missed Revenue & User Targets, CFO Flags Compute Contract Risk — AI Infrastructure Basket Sells Off
narratives.summary: OpenAI's internal growth miss triggers a confidence crisis in the entire AI capex supercycle thesis — directly hammering AI infrastructure dependents (Oracle -7.5%, CoreWeave, AMD, Nvidia) and threatening hyperscaler earnings guidance from MSFT, AMZN, META, and GOOG reporting tomorrow night, with spillover pressure on Bitcoin as risk appetite contracts.

narratives.headline: Strait of Hormuz Remains Disrupted as U.S.-Iran War Enters Month Two — Brent Tops $111, WTI Breaches $100
narratives.summary: Ongoing Hormuz disruption has Brent crude at $111.50 and WTI at $100, reigniting stagflation fears that keep the Fed on hold, compress margins for energy-intensive sectors, and force a defensive rotation — with Trump's rejection of Iran's ceasefire proposal extending the duration risk premium in oil and suppressing risk assets including Bitcoin.

narratives.headline: Hyperscaler Earnings Gauntlet Begins Tomorrow: Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Alphabet All Report on Same Night
narratives.summary: The simultaneous Q1 reporting by all four mega-cap AI spenders creates a binary event risk for the entire equity market — strong AI monetization data could reverse today's OpenAI-driven selloff and re-rate the Nasdaq, while any guidance cut or capex pullback would validate the AI overspend narrative and trigger a broad tech de-rating cascade.

narratives.headline: UAE Announces OPEC Exit Effective May 1, Reshaping Global Oil Supply Dynamics
narratives.summary: The UAE's departure from OPEC beginning May 1 fractures cartel coordination at the worst possible time — potentially unleashing unilateral UAE production increases or, conversely, removing a moderating voice, injecting structural uncertainty into oil price formation that ripples into inflation expectations, central bank policy paths, and energy equity valuations globally.

narratives.headline: Fed Decision Tomorrow: Powell Faces Stagflation Squeeze as Oil-Driven Inflation Collides With Slowing Growth Signals
narratives.summary: With rates widely expected on hold but energy-driven CPI re-accelerating and the BoJ simultaneously cutting its GDP forecast to 0.5% while raising inflation to 2.8%, Powell's press conference tone on the rate cut timeline is the single most rate-sensitive event of the week — a hawkish signal would spike yields, punish duration and growth equities, and pressure Bitcoin below key $76K support.

PANW.transmission: OpenAI revenue miss triggers broad AI infrastructure selloff → risk-off rotation compresses valuation multiples across high-growth tech → PANW, trading at elevated forward P/E as a cybersecurity growth name with AI-platformization thesis, faces multiple compression and sentiment-driven selling despite no direct AI capex exposure, compounded by hawkish Fed hold signaling higher-for-longer rates that disproportionately discount long-duration growth equities like PANW
PANW.key_narrative: WSJ Report: OpenAI Missed Revenue & User Targets, CFO Flags Compute Contract Risk — AI Infrastructure Basket Sells Off
